pattern: Precomputing the frequency of every pairwise AND value lets a single pass over the third element count zero-AND triples without enumerating all cubed combinations.
hints: Counting ordered triples directly is cubic; notice that the values are small, so the AND of any two of them lives in a bounded range.
Tabulate how many ordered pairs (i, j) produce each possible AND value first.
Build a frequency map of nums[i] & nums[j] over all ordered pairs, then for each nums[k] add up the counts of every pair-value whose AND with nums[k] is zero.

edge_inputs.description: single zero element gives one triple
single non-zero element gives no triple
all zeros so every ordered triple counts
powers of two never AND to zero among themselves but do with disjoint bits
overlapping high bits limit zero-AND triples
value with all low bits set plus its complement

stress.description: moderate array of small-bit values so many pairwise ANDs collide
stress.note: small value ceiling keeps the oracle cubic pass fast while exercising bit overlaps
